title: cheiranthus × cheiri subsp. fruticulosus
intro: Cheiranthus × cheiri subsp. fruticulosus, also known as the fragrant or rock wall cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its content of isothiocyanates. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its anti-inflammatory properties may also help in reducing pain and swelling. Cheiranthus × cheiri subsp. fruticulosus can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
